label: Parametric memory

node.label: Parametric memory
node.definition: Knowledge is encoded implicitly in learned parameters and recalled through model computation.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Parametric memory is modeled as depending on Large-scale pretraining, Transformers and feeding downstream nodes such as Semantic memory, Knowledge editing & memory consolidation.
examples: atlas downstream link to Semantic memory
atlas downstream link to Knowledge editing & memory consolidation
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Large-scale pretraining, Transformers.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Parametric memory has a `uses` dependency on Large-scale pretraining because Large-scale pretraining is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Parametric memory has a `uses` dependency on Transformers because Transformers is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Parametric memory has a `retrieves_from` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations with `common` strength.
